title: knema scortechinii
intro: Knema scortechinii, also known as the Borneo nut or Malayan nut, is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical rainforests of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain a high concentration of ricinoleic acid, a fatty acid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Knema scortechinii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve skin health, and support immune function. The seeds are often harvested and processed into herbal preparations, including oil and tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
